revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Amasashihikonomikoto-jinja]][[ja:天佐志比古命神社]] {{Expand Japanese|天佐志比古命神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine | native_name = 天佐志比古命神社 | image = 一宮神社の本堂.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.013739|133.041971}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine'''|天佐志比古命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Oki Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11659043|ltq=Q11659043|qid=Q11659043|1=List of Shikinaisha in Oki Province}} ** Series ordinal: 6 ** P155: {{ill|Make-no-Mikoto Shrine|lt=Make-no-Mikoto Shrine|qid=Q135041140|1=Make-no-Mikoto Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135041141|ltq=Q135041141|qid=Q135041141|1=Nakirahimeno- Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Chibu, Shimane|lt=Chibu|ceb|Chibu Mura|en|Chibu, Shimane|es|Chibu (Shimane)|fa|چیبا، شیمانه|fr|Chibu|id|Chibu, Shimane|it|Chibu|ja|知夫村|ko|지부촌|pl|Chibu|ru|Тибу (село)|sv|Chibu|tl|Chibu, Shimane|tr|Chibu|uk|Тібу|zh|知夫村|qid=Q1204968|1=Chibu, Shimane}} * {{ill|Q1063829|ltq=Q1063829|qid=Q1063829|1=Oki Province}} * {{ill|Chibu district|lt=Chibu district|ja|知夫郡|zh|知夫郡|qid=Q11584565|1=Chibu district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.013739|133.041971}} == image (P18) == * 一宮神社の本堂.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182997 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Son-sha|lt=Son-sha|ja|村社|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Unranked|lt=Unranked|ja|无位|qid=Q11504610|1=Unranked}} * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == country (P17) == * Улас|ca|Japó|canews|Categoria:Japó|caquote|Japó|cbk_zam|Japón|cdo|Nĭk-buōng|ceb|Hapon|ce|Ипнойчоь|chr|ᏣᏩᏂᏏ|ch|Chapan|chy|Japan|ckb|ژاپۆن|co|Giappone|crh|Japon|csb|Japòńskô|cs|Japonsko|csnews|Kategorie:Japonsko|csquote|Japonsko|csvoyage|Japonsko|cu|Ꙗпѡнїꙗ|cv|Япони|cy|Japan|dag|Japan|da|Japan|de|Japan|denews|Kategorie:Japan|dequote|Japan|devoyage|Japan|din|Japan|diq|Japonya|dsb|Japańska|dtp|Jepun|dty|जापान|dv|ޖަޕާނު|dz|ཇ་པཱན|ee|Japan|el|Ιαπωνία|elnews|Κατηγορία:Ιαπωνία|elquote|Ιαπωνία|elvoyage|Ιαπωνία|eml|Giapòṅ|en|Japan|ennews|Category:Japan|enquote|Japan|envoyage|Japan|eo|Japanio|eonews|Kategorio:Japanujo|eoquote|Japanio|eovoyage|Japanio|es|Japón|esnews|Categoría:Japón|esquote|Japón|esvoyage|Japón|et|Jaapan|etquote|Jaapan|eu|Japonia|ext|Japón|fa|ژاپن|fanews|رده:ژاپن|faquote|ژاپن|favoyage|ژاپن|ff|Japan|fiu_vro|Jaapan|fi|Japani|finews|Luokka:Japani|fivoyage|Japani|fj|Japani|fo|Japan|frp|Japon|frr|Jaapan|fr|Japon|frnews|Catégorie:Japon|frquote|Japon|frvoyage|Japon|fur|Gjapon|fy|Japan|gag|Yaponiya|gan|日本|ga|An Dineʼé Bikéyah|ny|Japan|oc|Japon|olo|Japounii|om|Jaappaan|or|ଜାପାନ|os|Япон|pag|Hapon|pam|Hapon|pap|Hapon|pa|ਜਪਾਨ|pcd|Japon|pcm|Japan|pdc|Japan|pfl|Japan|pl|Japonia|plnews|Kategoria:Japonia|plquote|Japonia|plvoyage|Japonia|pms|Giapon|pnb|جاپان|pnt|Ιαπωνία|ps|جاپان|pt|Japão|ptnews|Categoria:Japão|ptquote|Japão|ptvoyage|Japão|pwn|Dripun|qu|Hapun|rm|Giapun|rmy|Japan|rn|Yapon|roa_rup|Japonia|roa_tara|Giappone|ro|Japonia|ronews|Categorie:Japonia|rovoyage|Japonia|rue|Японія|ru|Япония|runews|Категория:Япония|ruvoyage|Япония|rw|Ubuyapani|sah|Дьоппуон|sat|ᱡᱟᱯᱟᱱ|sa|जपान्|scn|Giappuni|sco|Japan|sc|Giapone|sd|جاپان|se|Jáhpan|sg|Zapöo|shn|မိူင်းၵျႃႇပၢၼ်ႇ|sh|Japan|simple|Japan|si|ජපානය|skr|جاپان|sk|Japonsko|sl|Japonska|smn|Jaapaan|sm|Iapani|sn|Japani|so|Jabaan|sq|Japonia|sqnews|Kategoria:Japoni|srn|Japan|sr|Јапан|srnews|Категорија:Јапан|srquote|Јапан|ss|IJapani|stq|Japan|st|Japane|su|Jepang|sv|Japan|svnews|Kategori:Japan|svvoyage|Japan|sw|Japani|syl|ꠎꠣꠙꠣꠘ|szl|Japůńijo|szy|Zipun|ta|யப்பான்|tanews|பகுப்பு:ஜப்பான்|tay|Gipun|tcy|ಜಪಾನ್|tdd|ᥛᥫᥒᥰ ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Commons category (P373) == * Amasashihikonomikoto-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県隠岐郡知夫村1018', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '島根県隠岐郡知夫村郡', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Amasashihikonomikoto-jinja}} [[JA:天佐志比古命神社]] {{wikidata link|Q127783765}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine (Ichinomiya Shrine) |Image = [[File:Ichinomiya Shrine main hall.jpg|280px|Worship hall of Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|qid=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}{{ill|lt=Chibu Village|Q1204968|qid=Q1204968|1=Chibu, Shimane}}1018 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Amasashihikonomikoto |Sacred object = {{ill|lt=Bronze mirror|Q1041984|qid=Q1041984|1=Bronze mirror}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (minor shrine)|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = Before the 15th year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} (848) |Main hall style = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} variant |Other names = Ichinomiya Shrine, Ichinomiya-san |Pilgrimage designation = |Annual festival = July 15 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Amasashi
Hikonomikoto Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine''' (あまさしひこのみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Chibu Village|Q1204968|qid=Q1204968|1=Chibu, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|qid=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. === Overview === Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} and is considered one of the seven shrines of Chibu (Chibu Shichiza) {{Sfn|Oki History and Folklore Research Association|1998|p=124}}. Within the village it is called {{ruby|Ichinomiya Shrine|いちのみやじんじゃ}} or “{{ruby|Ichinomiya-san|いっくうさん}}” {{Sfn|Chibu Village Elementary and Junior High School Teachers’ Association|1990|p=100}}. There is a theater on the shrine grounds where kagura and children’s kabuki are performed {{Sfn|Globe-Trotter Editorial Office|2023|p=37}}. Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine and Chibu Village Gun District and Chibu Village Elementary and Junior High School 2.jpg|Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine, the Gun district, and {{ill|lt=Chibu Village Municipal Chibu Elementary and Junior High School|Q109363695|qid=Q109363695|lt=Chibu Elementary and Junior High School|1=Chibu Village Elementary and Junior High School}} Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine and Chibu Village Gun District 2.jpg|Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine and the Gun district === Enshrined deity === ; {{ruby|Amasashihikonomikoto|あまさしひこのみこと}}{{Sfn|Chibu Village Elementary and Junior High School Teachers’ Association|1990|p=100}} : The deity’s name means a male god who protects a bay—“the place where the sea (ama) cuts in” {{sfn|Toshinoya|1987|p=22}}. He is also known as {{ruby|{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}|おおなむちのみこと}} or {{ruby|Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|おおくにぬしのみこと}}, and is considered the same deity under different names {{Sfn|Oki History and Folklore Research Association|1998|p=124}}. === History === === From foundation to the medieval period === [[File:Cows and view from Chiburijima.jpg|thumb|The uninhabited island in the background is Kamijima]] The shrine is believed to have been founded before {{ill|lt=848|Q30380|qid=Q30380|1=848}} , and traditions are recorded in the *Shoku Nihon Kōki* and *Oki Tōshi*. The main points are as follows {{Sfn|San’in no Kamigami Publishing Association|2011|p=69}}{{Cite web |ja |url=https://www.pref.shimane.lg.jp/tourism/tourist/kankou/oki_shoukai/sightseeing/pamphlet/intdex.data/OkiJinjaMap_Dogo.pdf |title=Oki Dōzen / Dōgo Shrine Map |format=PDF |publisher=Oki Geopark Promotion Organization |accessdate=2024-06-27}}. * It is said that during the reign of the 32nd emperor, {{ill|lt=Emperor Yōmei|Q349281|qid=Q349281|1=Emperor Yōmei}}, Amasashihikonomikoto descended upon Kamijima, an uninhabited island of Chibu Village, and resided there for 59 years {{Sfn|Oki History and Folklore Research Association|1998|p=124}}{{Sfn|San’in no Kamigami Publishing Association|2011|p=69}}. * It is also recorded that in {{ill|lt=654|Q31109|qid=Q31109|1=654}}, during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|qid=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}} in the 4th year of {{ill|lt=Hakuchi|Q540118|qid=Q540118|1=Hakuchi (era)}}, the shrine was transferred to Higashihama in the Niburi area {{Sfn|Oki History and Folklore Research Association|1998|p=124}}{{Sfn|San’in no Kamigami Publishing Association|2011|p=69}}. It then remained enshrined in the Niburi area for over 1,000 years {{Sfn|Oki History and Folklore Research Association|1998|p=124}}. The earliest documentary appearance is in the *{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}*, which records that in the 15th year of {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} (February 848), the shrine was granted the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade {{sfn|Takahashi et al.|1996|p=474}}. The *{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}* lists it as the minor shrine “Amasashihiko Shrine” {{sfn|Takahashi et al.|1996|p=474}}, and the *{{ill|lt=Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11420490|qid=Q11420490|lt=Oki Province Jinmyōchō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register}}* records it as “Senior First Rank Amasajihiko Daimyōjin” {{sfn|Matsuura|1983|p=951}}{{efn|However, the “Senior First Rank” in the *Oki Province Jinmyōchō* is thought to have been manipulated by the {{ill|lt=Oki clan|Q11387649|qid=Q11387649|lt=Oki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Oki clan}}, rather than being a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen Senji|1=UNKNOWN}} issued by the {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|qid=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} {{sfn|Matsuura|1983|p=951}}.}}. When {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|qid=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}} was exiled to Oki in {{ill|lt=1332|Q6001|qid=Q6001|1=1332}}, he visited Chibu Village and worshiped at Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine {{Sfn|Oki History and Folklore Research Association|1998|p=124}}. === Early modern period === [[File:Ikku-jinja kyushachi niburi.jpg|thumb|Former shrine site in Niburi]] In the *Shimazen Muramura Jinmyōki* of the 16th year of {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} ({{ill|lt=1703|Q6822|qid=Q6822|1=1703}}), the shrine is referred to as “Amasashihiko-no-Mikoto Shrine alias Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto, Ichinomiya Daimyōjin of Chibu Village,” indicating that it came to be called “Ichinomiya” in the early modern period, meaning “the ichinomiya of Chibu Village” {{sfn|Takahashi et al.|1996|p=474}}{{efn|At this time, only the *Shimazen Muramura Jinmyōki* explicitly identifies Ichinomiya Shrine with the “Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine” of the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō* {{sfn|Matsuura|1983|p=952}}. The *Jinja Kakuroku* states “Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine (omitted) location unknown” {{sfn|Matsuura|1983|p=952}}. Toward the end of the Edo period, multiple works such as the *Inshū Kokishū* and the *Shingi-shi* section of the *{{ill|lt=Dai Nihonshi|Q1156003|qid=Q1156003|1=Dai Nihonshi}}* began to identify Ichinomiya Shrine with Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine {{sfn|Matsuura|1983|p=952}}.}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|qid=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1659|Q6995|qid=Q6995|1=1659}}), the shrine was moved from the Niburi (Niifuri) area to its current location in the Gun area {{sfn|Takahashi et al.|1996|p=474}}{{efn|A ridge tag also bears the inscription “Auspicious day, October, Manji 2 (Kinoe-I)” {{sfn|Takahashi et al.|1996|p=474}}.}}. During the {{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|qid=Q1146161|1=Meiwa}} era, following the stranding of a whale, children began dedicating hand dances {{Sfn|San’in no Kamigami Publishing Association|2011|p=69}}. From around the {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|qid=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} era these dances evolved into forms closer to {{ill|lt=kabuki|Q199701|qid=Q199701|1=Kabuki}} {{Cite book|ja|author=Shimane Prefectural Board of Education |title=History of Shimane Prefecture |publisher=Hōkōsha |date=1973-3-25 |page=344}}{{Full|date=July 2024}}{{efn|It is said that during the Bunsei era, a man named Ryōsaku from Tasawa in Chibu Village choreographed and taught theatrical dances {{sfn|Takahashi et al.|1996|p=474}}.}} and by 1998 had become close to the current form of village theater {{Sfn|Oki History and Folklore Research Association|1998|p=125}}. === Modern period === {{Questionable|The year {{ill|lt=1969|Q11185774|qid=Q11185774|1=1969}} (Shōwa 39)|date=2024-07}} A reconstruction {{ill|lt=sengū|Q2271023|qid=Q2271023|1=Sengū}} festival was held {{Sfn|Chibu Village History Compilation Committee|1996|p=851}}. === Shrine grounds === ; Stone where Emperor Go-Daigo sat : A stone said to be where {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|Q313942|qid=Q313942|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, exiled to {{ill|lt=Oki Province|Q1063829|qid=Q1063829|lt=Oki|1=Oki Province}} in the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Genkō|Q1194556|qid=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} ({{ill|lt=1332|Q3844595|qid=Q3844595|1=1332}}), sat to rest when he worshiped at Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine {{Sfn|Chibu Village History Compilation Committee|1996|p=851}}. ; Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine Theater : A {{ill|lt=rural kabuki stage|Q22124550|qid=Q22124550|1=Rural Kabuki Stage}} with a {{ill|lt=revolving stage|Q253227|qid=Q253227|1=Revolving stage}}, featuring stone embankment-style seating at the front; designated a village cultural property {{Cite web |ja |url=http://www.vill.chibu.lg.jp/gyosei/about/9 |title=List of Cultural Properties |publisher=Chibu Village |accessdate=2024-06-29}}. Community festivals such as Minai Odori are held here {{Sfn|Oki History and Folklore Research Association|1998|p=124}}. ; Tōshō Daigongen : Origins unknown {{Sfn|Chibu Village History Compilation Committee|1996|p=852}}. ; Wrestlers’ monument : Bears the inscription “Meiji 15: Tatsunami Yashichi, Wakagumi, Manriki Yonekichi, Chūren,” with the names of two wrestlers {{Sfn|Chibu Village History Compilation Committee|1996|p=856}}. Both were {{ill|lt=sumo wrestlers|Q2727289|qid=Q2727289|1=Rikishi}} from the Gun district, commemorated for their great success {{Sfn|Chibu Village History Compilation Committee|1996|p=856}}. ; Ebisu-san : An {{ill|lt=Ebisu|Q1129330|qid=Q1129330|1=Ebisu (mythology)}} statue with the inscription “2nd year of {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|qid=Q1146125|1=Kaei}}, Kaizen of Shōyō-ji” {{Sfn|Chibu Village History Compilation Committee|1996|p=855}}. ; Seishin Sakō Monument : Inscribed with “Do you not know the prowess of Japanese soldiers? Governor of Shimane Prefecture {{ill|lt=Fukumura Masaki|Q11594448|qid=Q11594448|1=Masaki Fukumura}}” {{Sfn|Chibu Village History Compilation Committee|1996|p=859}}. It commemorates Sugiyama Yasuichi from the Ōe district, who was killed in action in the 7th year of {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}; even after being wounded by a spear, he continued fighting while loudly proclaiming the words on the monument, leading to its erection in praise of his spirit {{Sfn|Chibu Village History Compilation Committee|1996|p=859}}. Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine (Ichinomiya Shrine).jpg|Torii gate Ichinomiya Shrine seen from the side.jpg|Main hall Chibu Village Ichinomiya Shrine theater.jpg|Theater Stone where Emperor Go-Daigo sat.jpg|Stone where Emperor Go-Daigo sat Tōshō Daigongen.jpg|Tōshō Daigongen Seishin Sakō Monument (Chibu Village).jpg|Seishin Sakō Monument Ebisu-san (Chibu Village).jpg|Ebisu-san Wrestlers’ monument (Chibu Village).jpg|Wrestlers’ monument Chibu Village Ichinomiya Shrine child-lion dance.jpg|Child-with-parent lion dance Ikku-jinja hengaku.jpg|Plaque inside the worship hall === Events === [[File:Ichinomiya Shrine Grand Festival.jpg|thumb|Ichinomiya Shrine Grand Festival]] ; Minai Odori : A dance dedicated on the 15th day of the 8th month of the lunar calendar to pray for a good harvest {{Sfn|Oki History and Folklore Research Association|1998|p=128}}. It is performed to the singing of “Hā—everyone line up together, let us show you the dancers’ movements, sā-sasasa” {{Sfn|Oki History and Folklore Research Association|1998|p=128}}. Designated an intangible cultural property of the village . ; Ichinomiya Shrine Grand Festival : Held every two years (even-numbered years) in late July {{Sfn|Oki History and Folklore Research Association|1998|p=124}}. Theatrical offerings are presented {{Sfn|Oki History and Folklore Research Association|1998|p=124}}. Performances such as {{ill|lt=Sanbasō|Q11356796|qid=Q11356796|1=Sanbasō}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiranami Gonin Otoko|1=UNKNOWN}} are staged by junior high school students {{Sfn|Oki History and Folklore Research Association|1998|p=124}}. The origin of the performances dates to the {{ill|lt=Meiwa|Q1146161|qid=Q1146161|1=Meiwa}} era of the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, when after a whale stranding and a subsequent epidemic in the village, children’s hand dances were dedicated {{Sfn|San’in no Kamigami Publishing Association|2011|p=69}}. ; Ten-sha pilgrimage : In Chibu there is a custom called the Ten-sha pilgrimage, in which people visit ten shrines in the village to pray for the success of surgery or to mark the end of mourning after a death {{Sfn|Chibu Village History Compilation Committee|1996|p=765}}. Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine is one of these ten shrines {{Sfn|Yama|2020|p=43}}{{Sfn|Chibu Village History Compilation Committee|1996|p=766}}. Scenes of Minai Odori.jpg|Minai Odori Amasashihikonomikoto-jinja Sengu Festival 1937.jpg|1937 Sengū Festival === Cultural properties === === Village-designated cultural properties === ; Tangible cultural property : Amasashihiko Shrine Theater – designated April 1, 1995 (Heisei 7) {{efn|Prior to this, the theater had been designated a “tangible cultural property” by the Shimane Dōzen Board of Education in 1973 (Shōwa 48) {{sfn|Takahashi et al.|1996|p=474}}.}}. ; Intangible folk cultural property : Minai Odori – designated November 1, 2001 (Heisei 13) . === Access === * 5 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Kurii Port|Q11523684|qid=Q11523684|1=Port of Kurii}} {{Cite web |ja |url=https://e-oki.net/okiraku/#/pc?pNo=1 |title=Oki-raku: Oki Tourism Guide 2023 Digital Pamphlet |format=PDF |publisher=Oki Geopark Promotion Organization |date=2022-04-17 |accessdate=2024-06-27}}. === Footnotes === === Notes === {{notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Further reading === * {{Cite book |ja |author=Oki History and Folklore Research Association |title=A Walk Through the Land of Oki |publisher=Harvest Publishing |date=1998-10 |isbn=4938184184 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book |ja |author=San’in no Kamigami Publishing Association |title=Gods of San’in: Visiting Ancient Shrines—A Pilgrimage Through the Mythic Homeland of Inaba, Hōki, Izumo, Iwami, and Oki |publisher=Imai Publishing |date=2011-06-20 |isbn=978-4-901951-59-3 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book |ja |editor1=Takahashi Tadashi |editor2=Suishi Shigekichi |editor3=Itō Yukio |title=Shinkoku Shimane |edition=Reprint |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Shrine Association |year=1996 |ref={{sfnref|Takahashi et al.|1996}}}} * {{Cite book |ja |author=Globe-Trotter Editorial Office |title=Oki |edition=3rd revised |publisher=Globe-Trotter |series=Globe-Trotter Island Travel 09 |date=2023-06-06 |isbn=978-4-05-802041-8 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book |ja |author=Chibu Village Elementary and Junior High School Teachers’ Association |title=Local Studies Materials: Chibu |publisher=Chibu Village Elementary and Junior High School Teachers’ Association |year=1990 |id={{NDLSearch|R100000001-I32111100333106}} |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book |ja |editor=Chibu Village History Compilation Committee |title=Newly Revised History of Chibu Village |publisher=Chibu Village |date=1996-12-01 |ncid=BA37098855 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book |ja |author=Toshinoya |chapter=51. Amasashihikonomikoto |title=Gods of Shimane |publisher=Shimane Prefecture Shrine Association |id={{JP number|88012198}} |year=1987 |page=22 |ref=harv }} * {{Cite book |ja |author=Matsuura Yasumaro |chapter=6. Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine |editor=Shikinaisha Research Association |title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: San’indō 4 |publisher=Kōgakkan University Press |year=1983 |pages=949–953 |id={{JP number|83032918}} |ref={{sfnref|Matsuura|1983}}}} * {{Cite journal |ja |author=Yama |date=2020-03 |title=On the “Ten-sha Pilgrimage” of Chiburijima |journal=Cultural Properties of Oki |number=37 |pages=43–51 |publisher=Oki Town Board of Education |id={{JP number|00077972}} |ref={{SfnRef|Yama|2020}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shrines in Shimane Prefecture|Q11476445|qid=Q11476445|1=List of Shrines in Shimane Prefecture}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Oki Province|Q11659043|qid=Q11659043|1=List of Shikinaisha in Oki Province}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Amasashihikonomikoto-jinja}} * [https://www.oki-geopark.jp/geopark-sites-features-list/1295/ Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine] – Oki UNESCO Global Geopark (Oki Geopark Promotion Organization) * [https://www.e-oki.net/spots/6008/ Amasashihikonomikoto Shrine] – Oki Island Travel (Oki Geopark Promotion Organization) == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amasashihiko-no-Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amasashihiko-no-Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amasashihiko-no-Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:知夫村]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]]
